audit_result.proof_trace: auctionCallback decodes the caller-supplied Call[] from data and passes it verbatim to _multiCall, which executes each Call with no restriction on target address or calldata. Because any AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE holder can construct a solution containing Call({target: tokenAddress, data: transfer(attacker, balance)}), they can drain any token held by the contract during the callback. The withdraw() function requiring DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE confirms this privilege was never intended for AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE.
audit_result.attack_scenario: An AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE holder calls bid() with solution=[Call({target: heldToken, data: abi.encodeWithSignature("transfer(address,uint256)", attacker, balance)})]; the auction transfers assetIn to AuctionBidder then invokes auctionCallback, which calls _multiCall executing the transfer and draining heldToken to the attacker before the auction can pull assetOut.

PROP-01b-partial2-inv-003.text: _multiCall in auctionCallback must not allow transferring contract token balances to arbitrary addresses.
PROP-01b-partial2-inv-003.assertion: forall solution in auctionCallback: no _call(target, data) where data encodes transfer(attacker, amount) on held tokens
